title: Family Medicine's Gender Pay Gap - PubMed
content: Full text links Cite Display options Display options Format Abstract PubMed PMID Abstract Female physicians earn less than their male counterparts, and many explanatory factors have been offered to account for these differences. An analysis of the 2019 American Board of Family Medicine New Graduate Survey Data demonstrates that women make 16% less than men, regardless of experience or hours worked. Keywords: Family Medicine; Gender Differences; Income; Primary Health Care; Surveys and Questionnaires; Women Physicians; Workforce. © Copyright 2022 by the American Board of Family Medicine. PubMed Disclaimer Conflict of interest statement Conflict of interest: none. Comment in The Gender Pay Gap in Family Medicine: Evidence and Next Steps. Paladine H, Dempster J. Paladine H, et al. J Am Board Fam Med. 2022 Jan-Feb;35(1):197-199. doi: 10.3122/jabfm.2022.01.210440. J Am Board Fam Med. 2022. PMID: 35039428 No abstract available.
